commands.review_summary:
The PR filters Barnacle's candidate labels so `proof: sufficient` suppresses negative proof-label additions on non-content PR events, and adds focused Barnacle tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: an external PR with `proof: sufficient`, missing body proof, and a non-content label event reaches `classifiedLabels` with `triage: needs-real-behavior-proof`; I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled PR with no narrow repair finding; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and merge decision.

target.title: fix(github): preserve sufficient proof against negative relabel
target.body:
Summary:
- Stop Barnacle from re-adding negative proof labels on non-content events when proof: sufficient is already present.
- Keep edited/synchronize proof invalidation unchanged.
- Add focused Barnacle coverage.

Example:
1. ClawSweeper adds proof: sufficient.
2. A later label event reruns Barnacle.
3. Barnacle's deterministic body-proof parser still sees missing proof.
4. Before this fix, Barnacle could re-add triage: needs-real-behavior-proof next to proof: sufficient.

Verification:
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts
- git diff --check
target.author: romneyda
